facts.name: Honey Never Expires
facts.content: Archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still perfectly edible. Honey's low moisture and acidic pH make it last basically forever.

facts.name: Octopuses Have Three Hearts
facts.content: An octopus has three hearts: two pump blood to the gills, and one pumps it to the rest of the body. When an octopus swims, the main heart actually stops beating — which is why they prefer crawling.

facts.name: A Day on Venus is Longer Than a Year
facts.content: Venus rotates so slowly that a single day on Venus (243 Earth days) is actually longer than a full year on Venus (225 Earth days). It also rotates backwards compared to most planets.

facts.name: Bananas are Berries, Strawberries Aren't
facts.content: Botanically speaking, bananas qualify as berries (they develop from a single flower with one ovary). Strawberries, on the other hand, are accessory fruits — not true berries at all.

facts.name: The Eiffel Tower Grows in Summer
facts.content: The iron structure of the Eiffel Tower expands in the heat, making it about 15 cm taller in summer than in winter. Thermal expansion is a real thing!

facts.name: Wombat Cubes
facts.content: Wombats are the only animals in the world that produce cube-shaped poop. Scientists believe the shape helps them stack their droppings to mark territory without it rolling away.
